lines.x: Rus, are you done packing?
Victoria's not like Kjerag. We're trying to work with the other side this time. Our standing's weak, and they WILL take advantage of that to tear us open.
The only thing I can tell you is, there's no way of guessing what they're going to do exactly. It's up to you to determine.
Alright, alright, I get it! You're going to chew my ear off, ugh!
If you have so little faith in me, why don't you do it yourself?!
Turn your brain on for once in your life and figure that out YOUR-self.
Why you...!
Alright, I've got no time to argue with you. Remember, this isn't an all-expenses-paid vacation. You're representing the Browntails.
Yucatan, keep an eye on your wife. Don't let her do anything stupid that would embarrass our clan.
Haha...
Don't nod to that! Yucatan, whose side are you on?!
Don't get sidetracked.
Short version: pay more mind on this one. Eyes out, ears out, mouth shut. Don't shoot off like you did before.
It's time you went out and broadened your horizons, anyway. Go and see the lands outside of Kjerag. See just what kind of place Victoria is.
Hmph...
Hey, you know... Ratatos.
What?
Is it me, or do you really wish you were going yourself?
Rus, over here.

Huh?
Hands to yourself, bitch! I don't have a temperature!
So why are you talking out of your ass?
I-I was just asking.
If you had the chance back then... would you have wanted to visit Victoria, Ratatos?
Ah, I finally found you two!
Too many guests just now, swarming me, I tell you! Pardon me for leaving you in the lurch.
Hardly, Mr. Harrison.
Thank you for inviting us. We've been having a delightful time.
Now, how nice of you!
I couldn't very well neglect you, not after you've come so far. Help yourself to any food or drink here, and if you need anything else, just fetch the closest servant.
That's very kind.
Oh, do you need me to quickly demonstrate how to use all this cutlery?
...We'll be fine.
Mr. Harrison, I think we should set that aside, and start discussing partnership arrangements between the Browntails and your own firm.
Ooh, very to-the-point, Madam Sciurus...
But I'm given to believe we've already reached firm consensus, haven't we, vis-a-vis partnership?
That is so.
But I believe we still need to work out the details of a few terms.
Of course, you have no need to worry whatsoever.
My assistant has the contract all ready. You can look over it whenever you have a moment to...
I've already read that contract.
Oh?
Let me be blunt. The Browntails cannot accept some of the terms within it.
Mm, hm...
So, your proposal, exactly?
We need to renegotiate the exact figures for provision of labor and revenue share.
Hmm.
Yes, I will consider your concerns.
So—
Hold your beasties, now, Madam Sciurus.
I'm of the mind that I've already been very generous with your cut on these orders.
But...!
Now, not to worry, this is still the same win-win for us, and I have a very good impression of Kjelac myself. I would never have you or your family come out of this with losses.
It's 'Kjerag,' not 'Kjelac,' Mr. Harrison.
Oh? Ah, of course, silly me, I always have trouble pronouncing that.
You've visited Kjerag before?
That... I can't say I have, but, quite some word of it has reached me in recent years.

I've heard they roast entire meatbeasts at Kjelac dinner parties, the wine comes in big jars as tall as you or me, and they put on variety shows and the like too. I shan't for the life of me miss a chance to see it myself.
You're joking.
Alright, I have to receive the other guests, now. Enjoy the party, my friends.
W—Wait...! I haven't finished—
Excuse me.
(Mr. Harrison, there you are! Who are those two over there?)
(Guests from Kjelac, wouldn't you know. They're from a fairly prestigious family in the area.)
(Ah, well, I've never heard of the place before...)
(It's nothing more than some far-flung mountains. Not worth your concern, Dame.)
We're leaving, Yucatan!
Rus...
Hrrrhhh...
Hffff... hrrrhhh...
Rus.
We're alone now.
(Deep breath)

AAAARRRGGHHHH HE PISSES ME OFF!!
HE PISSES ME OFF SO
HE PISSES ME OFF SO
MUCH!!
THE HELL IS HIS PROBLEM?! WHO LET HIM ACT SO HIGH AND MIGHTY AT ME?! YOU THINK I CAN'T TELL HOW MUCH YOU LOOK DOWN ON US, HUH?! ON KJERAG?!

HE WANTS TO TEACH ME HOW TO USE CUTLERY? EXCUSE ME? WHO CAN'T USE CUTLERY?! WHO PUTS A DUMB SHOW IN HIS DINNER PARTY? AND HE DOESN'T EVEN CARE TO SAY KJERAG RIGHT! IF WE WEREN'T GOING TO BE BUSINESS PARTNERS, ON KJERA-DAMNED-GANDR'S NAME, I WOULD PUNCH HIM INTO THE SUN!!
I AM SO! SO!! SO!!! FURIOUS!!!!
...*sigh*.
Here, drink some water.
No. My appetite is GONE.
Just drink some. Don't get those midnight stomach cramps.
You can massage me then!
Fine, sure. I'll massage you.
Rus, you did well to hold out so long.
Hmph. What was I gonna do otherwise?
If I went off right there, that bitch Ratatos would've jawed my head off later down the line.
You did well.
So where do we go from here?
Do you want to head back to the hall, or just go to bed for tonight?
Head back! Why wouldn't I head back?!
All we got out of this was me, furious! We still haven't finished discussing the contract! Don't you dare think this is enough to make me back down!
Really? Are you okay?
Uh, duh, I'm fine. I'm doing just great.
I didn't need Ratatos to tell me. I understand partnering here in Victoria is the Browntails' first step to contact with the outside world. I can't mess this up.
Not just that, I need every trick I up my sleeve to get us some good out of this.
So it's fine. I can bear it.
Rus...
Yucatan, you've seen everything I did the last two days—
I've never seen such big machines before. They look so clunky, but they run so smoothly.
And there were nice even streets all the way here, and stations everywhere, and the sheer stuff in the shop windows makes my eyes spin.
They don't even use candles for lighting this dinner party. It's electric lights...
Yucatan, Kjerag doesn't have any of this.
And I think we SHOULD have all this.
It just takes trains, pretty cities, stores in rainbow colors! All we've gotta do is build them!
And then our kids can grow up around it all, and then when they're big, they can study abroad, they can go be tourists... wherever they want to go, they'll be able to.
Um, you said 'kids'...
N-No! I didn't mean THAT! W-What are you thinking?! I meant, um, you know, Kjerag's future children...!
Calm down, calm down, Rus, it's not on my mind, it hasn't been—
What? You've never thought about us having kids before?!
I have... no, wait, not in that sense!
Ahem.
Basically, we need to do more, for the future, before we can rest.
That's what that stupid, horrible Enciodes thought as well, wasn't it?
All that roundabout crap was a little too complicated for me, but the railroads and factories that dipshit built... Kjerag's changed a lot thanks to them.
Rus...

What are you laughing at...?
Q-Quit patting my head! You're gonna mess my hair up!
Here, Rus, hold still. I'll sort it out for you.
I can't stand you... We're outside right now, you know!
Sorry, I couldn't stop myself.
You're right. These are things we should have.
Things we will have.
Of course!
But I never expected you to say it... or compliment Enciodes for once.
Ha, don't be stupid, Yucatan! I'd never say anything nice about the Silverashes!
I'm just being realistic here. Enciodes might make me sick, but if this is what he's been seeing all this time, then I can slightly understand all the stuff he's been doing.
I admit he's got a little skill, that's all. I'm magnanimous enough to say that.
It's true. Enciodes has finesse.
Besides, I hate that stupid Gnosis guy by his side way more than I hate Enciodes!
If it wasn't for that bastard pulling his pretend betrayal and scheming against us...
Under the circumstances, it was definitely a decent plan. Each side to their own, and none of the blame on him.
I don't care! What are you defending him for, Yucatan?!
I'm not...
(Glare)
Alright, that was my bad.
But it's been so long since it all blew over. When we make deals with the Silverashes in future, we're bound to run into their people again... When are you going to let go of your grudge, Rus?
When death pries it from my cold, dead hands.
Ugh.
Look, it's apples to pears! I'm not going to wreck Ratatos's business, but don't count on me forgetting what they've done!
...*sigh*.
Not that the Matriarch seems to mind at all anymore.
How, exactly?! That bitch might act like she doesn't care on the surface, but truth is? Her grudge runs deeper than mine!
Did you forget when we were kids and went out to play and we didn't call her along, and then Grandpa caught us when we came back and we had to write lines for a week?
I'll still bet anything that was Ratatos's revenge! She DID that!
You know we were playing outside for far too long, and that's why we were punished. We can't place the blame on the Matriarch.
Come on, you've got way too much faith in my sister!
You know, though, speaking of...
Yucatan, have you ever gotten the feeling she kind of admires Enciodes?
Not like she's said it, but I can vaguely sense it.
Hmm... I agree, actually.
If it wasn't for Enciodes's methods afterwards being too extreme, the Matriarch might not have reacted the way she did.
You think so too? Say... you think the reason she put me onto this one was—so I could see all this?
Before we left, I asked her if she would've wanted to go to Victoria in those days too, if she had the chance.
Really... I know how she's been thinking.
Ratatos! Ratatos!
Ratatos! If you're there, answer me! I've been yelling forever! I bet you're just pretending you can't hear me again!
Why are you ignoring m... huh? What are you staring at?
Why shouldn't I ignore a little scumbag who doesn't even invite her sister?
Who're you calling a scumbag?!
A little runt who sneaks out to play all by herself and still wants me to cover for her, that's who.
*cough* I'm... I'm sorry, that was my fault that time.
Next time I'll bring you! I'll get Yucatan to watch out for us!
Forget it, I'm busier than you anyway, and Grandpa's health is... whatever, who cares. Just don't stir up trouble for the next while, alright?
Also, stop depending taking advantage of Yucatan. You're practically bullying him.
How am I bullying him?
Because I said you are.
So. What do you call me?
Ratatos...
Huh?
The Firstborn! My elder sister!
That's better, dear sister.
Hmph...
Hold on, are you trying to trick me again? Don't dodge the subject. What are you looking at?
Just some periodic intel reports, nothing special.
Grandpa lets you do this stuff already? He really trusts you...
Because I'm capable of handling it, unlike my darling little sister.
Why you...!
Fine, I'm not picking a fight with you.
What kind of news is there this time? I mean, you've been staring at this forever...
Doesn't look like anything special...
Hm? The Silverash Firstborn's already left Kjerag?
He went to study at Victoria... Wow, he's great at excuses. I bet he couldn't stand it in Kjerag any longer, so he just ran to somewhere else!
Is that what you think?
That's what everyone says. If we can stay, then who'd want to go somewhere they don't even speak the same language? I know I wouldn't.
What? Ratatos... the Firstborn, what's with the funny look?
Stop. Put your hand away. I'm not at the point I need YOU to worry for me.
You bitch... I was being nice!
Enough. Thanks for being nice, then.
*sigh*...
It just fills me with something. It was all for the Silverashes' current situation, and him being able to push past his worries. That's what let him make a decision like this.
What decision?
The decision to abandon everything.
Enciodes... you really are a callous man.
That's what she said back then.
I forgot all about it, but when I saw her and that stupid Enciodes in the fire... I don't know why, and it was so many years ago, but it just instantly came back to me.
That's when it clicked. Ratatos wasn't occupied with any of the thoughts we thought she was.
Do you think... the Browntail clan has been a burden on her, this whole time?
Was this before the end of the Patriarch?
Yeah. Not long after that, Grandpa passed away.
You know now that Grandpa's health was already shot by then. Ratatos just kept the wool over our eyes...
Too many people had eyes on the Browntails, and the Matriarch was still too young. Some people were bound to get ideas.
Yeah. It got so busy for her she didn't even sleep. She had the heaviest bags under her eyes and thought I'd just miss them... Her temper got awful too. Ragging on me all day.
Yucatan.
Huh?
Back when I wasn't convinced, when I kept trying to disagree with Ratatos... I was being such a dumbass, wasn't I?
No—
Okay, stop, don't you say anything!
Mmph!
I know I was stupid then and I didn't help anything, but I sure ruined plenty! I was stupid to the max! It's fine, okay, I know all the way deep down!
It's whatever from anyone else, but you're the only one who can't say it.
You're not allowed to yell at me. I wouldn't be able to take it.
Rus... let me finish.
You're brilliant. I've never once thought you were stupid.
Ugh, you... How do you say this stuff so straight?
Hmm? Do I?
I don't think you're stupid, and neither does the Matriarch...
She totally does!
Alright... but she wouldn't fault you. You've always been trying to do a little something, and she's always trying to put faith in you. Haven't things turned out pretty great now?
I know that, I'm just still a little... angry.
Angry at myself, and angry at Ratatos.
Why couldn't I realize sooner, and why does she have to love showing off so much?
Well... do you want some real talk?
If it's not nice, don't say it.
Come on, that won't do.
Showing off probably runs in the Browntail family by now. You're not much different to her there, Rus.
So, our reluctant bravery-lover Madam Sciurus, do you have a plan to persuade that Mr. Harrison, and knock his greed down a few pegs?
I—
Hold on... what noise is that?
What a wretched dinner party.
Am I to believe Mr. Harrison has the courage to purchase a noble's land, but not the bravery to receive me as a guest face-to-face?
You lot.
Your orders.
Give our Mr. Harrison a little gift.
No cold feet to be had here. Teach this man how the mercantile should treat the aristocracy.
On it!
Argh!
Q—Quickly, fetch Mr. Harrison!
(What the hell? They're wrecking the whole hall.)
(Is that Victorian noble their leader? What is this meant to be...?)
(Looks like he and Mr. Harrison got into a mix-up.)
(He's destroying the house. That can't be your average mix-up.)
(Want me to take a look?)
(Hold on a bit longer. We can't just show up that casually. At least not right now!)
(But I think this might just be a good opportunity...)
Viscount Evans!
W—We're honored to have you! Why didn't you send word in advance? I heard you were holidaying in Leithanien; I hardly thought...
Mr. Harrison, you're quite the well-informed one on the subject of my travels.
You hardly thought I would return early. I imagine I've thrown a hardy spanner in your schemes, Mr. Harrison.
Tha—Tha... What are you...
Now, Mr. Harrison, none of that silly countenance.

You've been fraudulently snatching up shares in our joint firm, depressing prices and then purchasing the land on the sly while I was gone from the country. Not a good fit with your pathetic, wide-eyed look.
Viscount Evans, is this really called for?!
Our partnership is still in effect! If it's a question of interests, we can still hash things out again... This is hardly how you treat your joint associate!
But of course I haven't forgotten our partnership.
And yet still, I would dearly suffer for not letting off a little steam here.
(A dispute of interests between this merchant and this noble...)
(Ratatos pushed me through a bunch of material before we left, so I knew about this, but who knew I'd see it first-hand.)
(But with this...)
(Do you have an idea, Rus?)
(Yep. You just asked me if I had a plan for how to negotiate with Mr. Harrison.)
(Now I do—quick, Yucatan, here's our chance!)
You can't... You can't just do this!
Oh, can't I?
Mr. Harrison, let's be crystal-clear. Suppose I *do* do something to you! Then, they'll report that 'Pitiful Sir Harrison sadly passes away in duel with Viscount.'
Bahah, you can't possibly hope they'd lay hands on a noble?
(Sir.)
Huh?
(People got a lot of our guys knocked out now, only a few up still.)
(And seems one of the guests called the police. Look...)
You're not as dull as you suggest, Harrison. Fine job on finding some lackeys.
What...?
Enough of the tomfoolery!
I'd come in thinking there would be future leeway for discussion if you were tactful enough here, but it seems you don't quite agree.
Hmph. Today was just a lesson. We're far from through yet.
We're leaving!
They're just gone now?
Ugh... What about these guys, then?
The red-haired Zalak carelessly dumps the people over her shoulder in front of the magnate.
Just put them here. Someone will deal with them.
Ah... are these all—Viscount Evans's...
You don't mean those 'lackeys' he mentioned were—
If you don't have anyone else to choose from, then I imagine you mean us.
Are you alright, Mr. Harrison?
I escaped the worst of it, though I must seem an awful fool to the two of you, and, well, er...
No matter what, I thank you with all my soul.
Only, having set about to the Viscount's men like so, I worry in future, er... *sigh*...
Mr. Harrison.
Let me be straightforward about that—your partnership with that noble's in for a rocky ride, isn't it?
Well...
You've publicly offended him now we've beaten up his entourage, and it looks like he's a rung on the ladder above you, so you've got no way to get at him. Is he going to just keep working with you?
Based on what I know, your firm is in the key phase of its expansion right this moment, and if your partner pulls out midway, that'll be a massive blow to you, right?
Wh... What are you getting at here?
You can't be telling me you just did all that on purpose?!
Come on, I'm doing nothing more than talking facts here, that's all. Don't go and overthink it.
You're stuck between continuing to beg to that noble, or searching for a new associate under pressure. We have a small, little proposal there...
We Browntails are one of Kjerag's Three Clans. A single puny Victorian noble poses no threat to us. If you're inclined, the Browntails will be your most reliable business associates.
How about that, Mr. Harrison? Regarding that partnership between us—
—Is it me, or do you think we can renegotiate now?
Still a little chilly, so early in the morning.
Typical of Shangshu's weather. Cool now, hot and hotter by noon. It's a fickle one.
There is rule to heat and cold. I presume Shangshu's folk have long since accustomed.
Indeed. But seeing the rain come harder lately, I'm worried the weather's about to turn.
Best it isn't some blistering cold or heat, so the people have a little time to adapt.
The season is here. Shocking turns come as granted.
Boatman, may you ferry me?
By all means. Where to?
Across the river.
Fine by me. How should this river of yours be crossed, in your view?
Which route? Are you hurrying or taking your time?
What difference is there?
If you're in a rush, and time is short, I'll ferry fast. We'll reach the other shore without much effort.
So what if I am not?
Then we'll have to fix up some wine and sides before we cross.
The wine's warmed aboard the boat, and we come from upstream to the crossing, staying to the side as we drift down. You can gaze on the city, and see the fields being planted. Fine scenery.
Not half-bad.
Then so it'll be. I'll warm the wine now.
You're deft at business.
So this line of work goes. Always a need to play it by ear.
Few sirs like you would turn down viewing more of old Shangshu City. If it's not for the city, then it's for the people.
Quite.
The people deserve to be viewed. If not, then it's been a wasted journey.
I imagine Liang Xun has informed you—he is to go with me this evening to return to the capital. The way out of Shangshu will be led by you.
He did inform me.
Don't blame us. Mr. Liang's leaving in a rush here. Us folk can't help but be worried.
Our Magistrate... As good as a parent to us, we say, but he's still young in the end. Resembles the nephews of our own at times. There's concern to be had.
Liang Xun has the talent, and the resolve. Should he withstand, he will be famed and recognized.
And what if he doesn't?
—*sigh* No, never mind, I shouldn't ask. If you want to see Shangshu, just come with me.
Only shame is, young Lee's still searching for someone right now. He'll be hard-pressed to meet Mr. Liang this evening if he wants, I think.
'Tis still quite cool. 'Tis lucky we are not cold ourselves, with the tea warm.
Official affairs inundate you, my Lord. What brings you here so early?
I agreed to watch the play with you.
Cloak in some more layers. Mind the chill.
When did we agree this? All I had said was to come if you were free.
And I'm free today.
Liar. Your work is surely a mountain of bother, with the Grand Tutor so urgent in handing it over to you... Could it be, my Lord, you did not sleep last night?
*cough* I did get a little shuteye.
You're also early to rise today, Miss Ning.
I guessed the headstrong Liang would come at first light despite all exhortation, and so was awaiting you the whole time.
That. Hm. I...
Your tea cup is empty. Pass it to me.
Thank you.
'Tis but a cup of tea. Need we such formalities between us?
'Tis pleasant, to be able to sit here in this mountain, drinking tea, shaking oneself awake.
The refreshments at this shop are quite fine too... the shaobing, sesame cake, and—
Sweet fritters.
You've lauded this place before.
Ah, and you remembered?
Mm. Being that you're fond, I take note.
Everything of Shangshu is fine, save the slight strength in flavor. Lucky that the pastries by and large are tender and sweet still. They fit my palate.
*sigh* Your estate stews were very spicy at first, my Lord. Though, they have been considerably better since.
I could not pick up my chopsticks before needing to seek water. How very sorry for me.
The estate has spice-remedying decoction available at all times.
Miss Ning, your home...
The waterways of the south. We are no great spice eaters.
I apologize for the trouble.
What matter is it? We all must adapt at some point.
So you say, though it's not so easily done.
'Tis not hard, should you intend to.
We all must adapt at some point.
Verily.
You have mentioned you are native to Shangshu, my Lord?
I am.
I left home at an early age, and only returned in recent years. You know as much, Miss Ning.
I have heard you make mention.
Is it then that you met your Lungmen friend, in your period away?
Yes.
I knew nothing back then, left to forge my own way, made no shortage of friends, believed the spirit of youth could conquer everything, accomplish anything...
I did plenty of clownishly ridiculous, foolish things.
'Tis no foolish thing to make friends.
My Lord, the friend you have made is a fine one.
...I was simply lucky to.
Were it not for such urgent affairs, 'twould only be right we bid your friend on a true, proper tour of Shangshu, being the hosts that we should.
'Twould also be more chance to hear some anecdotes of your past, my Lord. Who knows how different you are these days?
I have never changed.
No...
Maybe I have changed some. But...
Hm?
What I set out to do, for these common peoples... has never changed.
When the Grand Tutor last evening proposed I accompany him to the capital... I didn't want to accept, at first.
You denounce me for accepting in your place?
No. I hardly could.
I should not have hesitated in the first place. I should be thanking you, Miss Ning.
So long as you do not blame me. I understand you are uneasy for Shangshu, as can others, but the Grand Tutor's word is a different story.
He keeps no mediocrity for company. What he assigns are but the most rugged, hard-fought opportunities.
I believe, should my perception of you not be faulty, that if you had refused in a moment of hesitation, you would surely regret it in time.
I understand... I apologize for worrying you.
...Heheh.
Miss Ning...?
Ah, no, nothing. I just suddenly imagined...
If you had then and there earnestly, unreservedly agreed to leave, I fear I would perhaps have been quite angry.
Ahem. You're joking, Miss Ning.
I am certainly not joking.
*cough* *cough* *cough*.
In so little time, the sun has come up.
My Lord, are you enjoying this play?
It's not bad.
Then shall you be descending the mountain sooner?
No.
I'll watch another while.
Miss Ning, watch another while.
Okay, come on, Dad! Stop looking at it!
Why? What's even the point? It's just a travel itinerary! Me and the guys already hashed it out! We're leaving once everything's packed!
And I have a whole ROOM of stuff that still needs packing, so!
What's the rush? Let me look over it again...
You're a group of young people. You rush your decisions. That's a recipe for disaster.
What's wrong with young people? You were all young once, right? Didn't you go all over when you were young, Dad?
Is that a fair comparison?
Without anyone to accompany you, will you be sure of which ways you can go, and which you can't?
For this long a journey in this season, do you even know what you should bring, and what you shouldn't?
Dad!
Times have changed! Stop lecturing me about your old days!
Yaoye! Listen, there's not much your dad can tell you, so...
If you're sure you're going to Yumen, then go. I couldn't stop you all anyway. Avoiding the path we laid out for you might just be for the better...
Dad, suppose, um, and I'm just picturing...
Suppose we don't run our inn here, you've still got a couple cousins. Have you considered going with us to Yumen?
Yaoye, ah...
Ugh, dad, I'm just wondering out loud, I know it's not gonna happen! You can just listen or something...
Yaoye.
...*sigh*.
This inn was meant to be left to you. If you're not taking it, we may as well close up shop sooner rather than later.
If you want to set up your own business, I won't stop you. I trust you to have thought it through. But I still need to tell you a thing or two, and I don't want you to think I'm prattling.
Dad...
You're leading all those youths. They trust you, follow you. You say times have changed... You're right, times have changed, but this sort of thing doesn't change no matter what the times are.
You step forward, and they're there all at once, so no matter how difficult it gets in future, you can't back down. No matter how tough, how exhausting, you have to bear it. Do you hear me?
I know.
I... I know, Dad.
...*sigh*. Alright, take the itinerary. I've kept you off of some of the tougher roads. If you can stick to the highways, do so. Never revisit those same muddy paths we waded through.
No need to worry.
It's hard, not worrying.
I have a worry, right this moment.
Go on.
Yaoye, you're not young anymore. When you go to Yumen, can you possibly try and bring back a decent young man...?
Stop, stop!
Dad! That is SO stupid to worry about!!
How is it stupid?
It just is!
Ugh, jeez, I'm not talking to you anymore. I need to go to Mr. Liang's place anyway.
What do you want Mr. Liang for?
I'm not the one looking for him, it's that stupid Lee guy!
If Lee can't find him, Mr. Liang's not likely to be at the estate. What use would you have for going?
That makes sense. Yeah, okay! He's just using the same trick again to dupe me! I'm gonna go find him and GET MINE!
*sigh*...
You proud little girl. When will you finally settle down...?
Do not pry into others' domestics.
Your concern is unnecessary.
Lin Qingyan, what do you mean by this?
Eat your food.
You know I'm not referring to that.
You were waiting here from sun-up, so you could hold me from visiting those three. You have to have some reason for me.
I told you, I'll go with you.
Lin Qingyan!
You're making a scene. Look at yourself.
I did not make this trip in my capacity with the Central Judicial Office. Disregarding our positions, what should you be calling me?
Are you forgetting the rule?
...Auntie.
Good.
Why do you always bring this out to stifle me? It's not as if you're an honest relative. You're just relying on the friendship between the Lins and my dad, and being a generation older.
Just why did the Tianshi Bureaus have you make this trip?
For the common people, naturally.
How nice for the common people.
If that's the case, will you really just ignore... will you really just carry on like this?
The Grand Tutor has made his judgment. I may simply await orders.
But you, Zuo Le.
Within the Sui Regulator, you're still green. What is concealed behind this trip to Shangshu is not simple, and as it seems now, you have no awareness of the inside details.
Dispatched yet barely cognizant. Ridiculous! Who called you here?
No one sent me. I volunteered myself.
What? Don't you believe me?
I do.
I believe you volunteered yourself, but I also believe there are strange actors behind this.
This is my first time hearing that the impartial, incorruptible, honest, forthright Lin Qingyan of the Central Judicial Office has learned to speculate on the bureaucracy's murky secrets.
Don't rumors say you're sick of giving those sorts of shameless busybodies the time of day? That if anyone tries to wag on at you, you shock their brain numb?
This, right now, is far from your style.
You have no need to point fingers at my style of affairs.
I'm entrusted with your care. When things concern you, it's only natural I exercise some caution.
...Tch.
Did you click your tongue at me?
I did not. You misheard, Auntie.
Once this matter is through, you should return with me to the capital as well.
But...
General Zuo desires it.
Understood. I will accompany you back.
Assistant Minister of Rites... 'tis not a post that participates too much in government affairs.
Did you ever hear, my Lord, how this position of mine came to be?
I'd like to.
You rose upon your own excellence; this I cannot compare to.
The Ning family... 'tis major in a sense. Sordid that its ancestors were, its descendants did progress. Generations accumulated propriety, connections and assets, and so things passed down.
My father's father was an official, first rank, assiduous for quite some decades. You should know him even if I did not tell you his name.
I would only be ready to say so to you, my Lord, lest it seem I was flaunting.
Hardly.
Miss Ning... you are a fine one.
I knew you would not think so.
I have told you, too, that I rank seventh within my own generation.
Good.
My father's generation sought otherwise, as did the one below. My oldest cousin is on my father's side, ten or so years my senior, and has long settled himself down.

As my grandfather prepared to retire from officialdom, so I was at junior vigor's peak, and became drawn into a smuggling and embezzlement case. By a fluke I caught a string of thieves, and the documents landed on my grandfather's desk the following day.
Your ability excels, Miss Ning.
Pleasantry.
I would never. It is my honesty.
Then I will believe you.
My grandfather called me to his study alone; we conversed of little in particular, before he asked me this one question.
He asked: 'Ciqiu, would you be willing to give your ability and learning for the country, the people, and for the realm?'
And I replied...
...You would offer your life.
Without hesitance.
Your spirit is bold, Miss Ning.
Enough, now. The story ends here.
My Lord, your tea is cold.
It's still somewhat warm.
But I notice your hands are cold now, my Lord. If you would be willing to have half this cloak back from me, 'tis not out of the question we could steep a freshly brewed pot.
Health is crucial. Wear it well. Don't take it off.
I am fine. If you do not believe, shall we test... if I am warmer than you?
My hands are cold. Don't be chilled, Miss Ning.
Aiya!
After today, I don't know when we'll find the chance to meet again.
Take more care of your health, Miss Ning. Don't let the cold tempt you.
Should...
Should you ever wish to view the flowers in future, and don't deem it too far-flung, then you needn't take it upon yourself. Have someone take a letter, and send them to me to plant.
Miss Ning?
Right, ah, um...
Well... Given you speak so, I will be straightforward too.
My Lord, how much longer are you going to keep that ribboned jewelry case in your pocket?
...Ahem. This is...
What, my Lord? Could you have possibly intended to give that to another?
Ugh, can't stand it.
We shouldn't have taken you to him.
Shouldn't have.
Won't you show yourself?
Wouldn't work, showing myself now.
Their whole back-and-forth's more of a work of art than any stage play.
Who knew old Liang would... *sigh* Never we mind.
No... That won't do. I do mind!
Who knew after a decade and change apart, Liang would turn out like this!
A decade and change apart. It's not uncommon.
It gets to me.
At least there's still tea to drink, and a play to watch.
Well, Liang Xun, you owe me another now.
*sigh*...
One last cup of wine with an old friend really is difficult to swing.
9:35. Five minutes late.
We'd better find another place. For safety.
I don't want to spend a second more than I have to, but you know what want even less? To miss our contact.
He's supposed to come straight to us first thing after getting into Lungmen, but there ain't a single soul coming out of city gate. He's probably stuck somewhere inside. So we wait.
Too many eyes here at the city gateway. You sure you wanna stick around?
What else are we gonna do? We gotta do what the psycho bitch wants!
You remember the first thing she said after she caught us?
'Changed my mind. Let's hope you're more useful to me alive than dead.'
Not that thing! The next one!
'Don't blow this. Mess up a single time, I cut your throats.'
So, so instead of risking missin' our guy and losin' the letter, I'd rather be a little more conspicuous.
You—Whatever.
There's the crowd.
What does the guy look like again?
Don't hurt yourself trying to remember. It's not going to happen.
What do you mean?
*sigh*.
The broad never said anything about what our contact looked like or what he's wearin'. He'll come to us, not the other way around.
You remember how the meeting goes down, right?
Gambino?
The contact asks us, 'Would you like some mayonnaise teppanyaki macaroni,' we'll tell him, 'Supersize, for two,' and he'll give us the letter.
I'm impressed.
Just hearing that password makes me wanna puke. Couldn't forget it even if I wanted to.
It's 9:50. Even the last group into the city is gone by now.
Something must've gone wrong. We should move.
What if he shows and we ain't here?
Like that could happen. We can't just stand around any longer!
Maybe customs busted him?
Even if not, no harm in waitin' for him a little longer. It's his own damned fault he didn't find us. The crazy bitch can't do nothing about that...
What's the difference?!
She's off her goddamned rocker. She ain't gonna think about who's at fault here, it's all about results! If you don't move your furry ass right now, you're signing our death certificates!
Alright, let's say I do it your way. Where do we look for him?
Use your brain.
Only a Siracusan would write the looney broad a letter. Aside from Penguin Logistics, who else in Lungmen's got anythin' to do with Siracusa?
You tellin' me it's the traitors we brought with us from the old country?
Not only did they betray us, they're nabbing our guy?
Heh, guess you do still got a couple of brain cells to rub together.
Can't say for sure it was them, but they do fit the profile. They're gonna know something, at least.
Andiamo.
Those traitorous cazzi. One day, I swear I'm gonna—
Hold it!
I've been telling you since the first day we stepped into Lungmen. They got their own rules here.
Don't forget how we lost to the Rat King, and how he let us go without a scratch.
Don't need the reminder, Capone!
I sure hope so.
So, we're droppin' in on the traitors' hideout. We ain't there to pick a fight or teach 'em a lesson. Just doin' a little information gathering. You hear me?
Slow down. This is the place.
How do you feel?
Never better.
What's the plan? Grab a couple and start askin' questions?
Probably a better way.
If they're the mooks who kidnapped our Messenger guy from Siracusa, then it's somethin' their bosses cooked up. And if it wasn't them, then these small timers ain't gonna know anything.
It's times like these you actually give that brain a little workout, huh?
So, how are we runnin' this one? Like we did in the old days? You go make some noise while I sneak in?
Not so fast. We gotta case the joint a little longer. Don't take a risk if their guard is low enough.
We're working for the crazy broad. I need all the help I can get, even if you usually do more harm than good.
Hah, listen to yourself. If it wasn't for me, you woulda screwed up enough for the psycho to run you through.
Haha, twenty-one!
Hell yeah, come to papa!
Hold on, what the hell's that?
What's what? You better pay up—
We got a cheat!
(Quietly) The lookouts are playin' cards in broad daylight?
(Quietly) If I were their boss, I'd shove the whole deck down their throats.
(Quietly) Save it. Who do you even think you are now?
(Quietly) Besides, it's pretty convenient for us they ain't payin' attention.
*Sigh*... I lost big this time.
Haven't been paid jack for a while now. Wouldn't've been playin' otherwise.
What, you think I'm loaded? You were tryin' to steal from me!

Your turf is close to the markets. At least you can pick up some odd jobs and run errands around there. Me? I gotta stroll around the Infected camp. Place's like a graveyard. No work at all, even if I tried looking for some!
Odd jobs?!
Who's there?!
What? What was that?
I heard someone say something just now, and pretty damned close.
What did they say?
I think it was... 'odd jobs?'
Hahahaha... You're hearin' things. Or some punk's tryin' to spook you.
Maybe.
(Quietly) Are you outta your mind?!
(Quietly) No...
(Quietly) Back when we picked up these kids, when we made them Gambinos, Sicilians...
(Quietly) Did ever think they'd end up... errand boys?
(Quietly) Outta my mind? What was all those years of blood, sweat, and tears for? What are they in this for?
(Quietly) To live another day, you egomaniac!
(Quietly) This is all good news, with the shape we're in. And you're complaining?
(Quietly) Of course I'm complaining! How am I supposed—
Shit! They're packing heat?!
No, that's an Originium explosion. A couple blocks away, from the sound of it—Where are you going?!
You wanna stay here your whole life?
Slow down!
G-Gambino, you're running too—
Capone suddenly finds himself speechless, just as Gambino did when he reached the area a dozen or so seconds ago.
A man wearing a black hat and raincoat stands in front of them.
A number of local Lungmenite triads lies on the ground around him. Some of them are unconscious, while others are groaning as they hold their wounds, but they are all alive.
Would you like some mayonnaise teppanyaki macaroni?
Yeah... Supersize, for two.
Buon appetito.
Capone walks a few steps forward and takes a letter from the man in black.
The wax seal on the envelope seems familiar, but he doesn't immediately recognize what it represents.
Buongiorno, which family do you—
Boss—No, Mr... Gambino?
Tommaso?!
What are you doing here—No, who gave you this letter? Where's the guy you got this from?
I-I don't know! The letter's from me!
I know what kind of man you are, Tommaso. You can't fool me.
I know you don't have what it takes to put all these men on the ground by yourself. They'd beat you half-dead. The letter man's gotta be the one who did this.
You'll tell me where he went, if you know what's good for you.
I... I can't.
Looks like you got pretty forgetful since I been gone.
Then I guess I'll gotta squeeze it outta you! You'll beg for death.
No!
I won't tell you, no matter what! Not even if you kill me!
Alright, you asked for it—
Gambino!
Since when did you get all sentimental? If we find the letter man, we might just get a way out of this godforsaken town, back to...
Look at the envelope, the seal. Don't tell me you don't know what that is.
It's... from her? Why would she... Why's she thinkin' about Lungmen?
Crap, it's the L.G.D.! They're here lookin' into the blast!
Tommaso, come with us—
He's gone?!
Don't worry about him. We're in enough trouble!
Drop your weapons... surrounded...
This way.
That's a dead end!
Trust me! Hurry!
I-Is this a gang war?
No, looks more like a one-sided power move... See? They're all alive.
You're right. They're hurt pretty damned bad, though.
The perp can't be far! Let's move!
Which way?
We have patrols on both ends of the street. If they had even half a brain, they'd have gone down this alley.
Hah, I know this alley. It's a dead end.
(Quietly) This is your idea of a way out?
(Quietly) Didn't you hear 'em? They got men posted on both ends of the street. We're trapped!
Alright, come on out. You can't hide!
Seeing as you didn't kill any of them, we'll go easy on you.
Stop resisting. Come out already!
(Quietly) They're gonna find us!
(Quietly) Shut up. I'm thinking. I'm thinking...
We know you're hiding behind that wall. Drop your weapons and come on out!
(Quietly) Got anything yet?
(Quietly) I'm thinking. I'm—
I'll say it again. Drop your weapons, and we'll go easy—
In your dreams!
Freeze—
Stop! Stop right there!
Now, make a run for it!
What? He's got an accomplice. Is this a diversion? Quick! Call for backup—
He's got at least one accomplice. They have crossbows. Terrible aim!
Terrible what?!
If this wasn't Lungmen, I woulda blown your brains out!
*pant*... *pant*...
They probably won't catch us here.
All we ever do these days is run, hmph. And all because of a few punks?
It's just like you to say that, you egomaniac.
That arrogant air of yours gets more intense the harder we're screwed. It pisses me off more than it did when we were young.
What? Takin' a trip down memory lane?
A little.
I still remember who gave me this last arrow wound here.
And if I weren't in the same boat as that cazzo right now, I'd give him one to match, a little closer to his heart. No point in going down memory lane otherwise.
Yeah, you're damned right.
And I'd love to stick another arrow in that arrogant bastard. The brain this time. I won't miss.
Hah, nice. If you wanna do me in so badly, why don't we get right down to it?
You think I'm scared of you?
What else could it be? You couldn't take me down, not without gettin' the jump on me.
(Deep breath)
Sure, I couldn't take you in a fight.
Oh? Finally admitting it?
Don't forget who we work for... You brought her up earlier, now it's my turn to remind you.
Looks like you're still sane enough to calm down. Thank God.
I'm still worried, though. After all, we didn't get the letter from the real contact. You think that crazy broad might say we messed up?
Hell would I know?
All I know is the two of us together wouldn't last ten seconds against her.
Ain't like we got a choice, so why worry about it?
I can smell you two through the door. You've been standing outside for a while. What's up?
N-Nothing, ma'am. All's well. Everythin's just fine. We got the letter.
Everything's fine?
Yeah, it all went really well. Great, even.
It all went well, but it took you this long to come back?
Show me the letter.
D-Done already? Y-You read it that fast?
Got a problem?
N-No, not at all. No problems with that. Not a single one. No opinions even.
Feels like you still have something to say, though?
What he's tryin' to say is... Does that letter talk about us at all?
Haha, hahahahaha.... HAHAHAHAHA!
Well?
Yeah.
The letter says I can deal with you two however I like.
What's the matter? No reaction to that bit of information?
Is... Is that really it?
Heh, never would've thought. You've got a nose for this sort of thing.
The letter says that, considering what absolute failures, what miserable embarrassments you two have been, I should dig a hole somewhere out in the barrenlands and leave you six feet deep.
You don't seem very surprised.
What's to be surprised about? Even I think we're embarrassing.
So, what'cha gonna do?
Take your own lives in disgrace?
You could try to make a run for it again. I'll give you fifteen whole seconds this time.
Don't joke about that. We know the score.
Heh.
Alright, I have another idea.
You two never actually meant all that much to them in the first place.
You know their rules, right? Stop fooling yourselves. Signora Sicilia isn't going to care about two rabid stray dogs. Hell, she doesn't even care about me.
You two know what that means. It's Siracusa itself that wants you two gone.
I don't really care much about that, but even I think you two desperate puppies are a pathetic sight.
So, why don't you duel it out?
That'll get you back some of that so-called honor, the one who wrote the letter ends up in a better mood, and I'll get me some quiet time.
How about it? Whaddya think?!
A blood pact, then...
What was that?
If we go against the letter's instructions, betray our own Family, and even make a pact with you, you'll only be able to protect the one who lives, right?
Pact? Protect? Betrayal?
Do I have to tell you guys how many times you've stabbed each other in the back? Me?
Alright... I accept.
Capone?!
Didn't you just tell me there's no point in going down memory lane?
Alright, I'm in. I'll count to three, and that's when we'll go. We'll see who lives.
And by the way, I didn't strike before I finished talkin'. That's me makin' it up to you for all those times I stabbed you in the back.
Alright, traitor. We'll call it square.
Tre...
Due...
Uno!
Fight!
Hahahaha... Hahahahaha! HAHAHAHA!
I love that!
More! Give me more! Free the hate in your hearts! Show me just how far you can go when your lives are on the line!
Gambino, now!
Graaaahhhh!!
Take this—
To the left!
No way... She didn't even draw her sword—
Amateurs. I know where your attacks are coming from without even really looking.
You make a good couple of brothers. The dumb one is dumb as hell, and the one that looks smart only looks it.
I bet you two didn't have it that hard back in Siracusa when you were little. Life must've been comfortable?
Gambino, don't give her the room to talk—
No way! Barehanded?!
Is that so surprising?
It's like we come from two totally different Siracusas.
Just like this.
How does it feel to have your own crossbow bolt pressed against your throat?
Can't say I'm feelin' a lot.
Since you didn't like my idea, I'll have to follow the letter and dispose of you two in whatever way pleases me.
You forgot about one thing.
What's that?
This is Lungmen.
So?
You can't cut a throat with rubber-tipped bolts!
I knew you had a knife on you. Didn't expect you to be so quick to draw it.
But still... too slow!
Gugh—
And sure, these bolts with rubber heads can't kill.
But it's not so hard to find your aorta.
*chokes*
Capone?!
Ugh... Guh—
*cough* *cough*!
Congrats. You saved your life.
*cough*... So what? You're still gonna...
Save it, Capone.
We should've died here a long time ago. Let's at least go out like real Sicilians.
Oh?
For a brief moment, Gambino and Capone think they are seeing things.
They see Lappland holding the exquisitely decorated envelope and the neatly unfolded letter above an open flame.
A corner of the letter lights up.
Are you nuts?! You... You burned Signora Sicilia's letter?!
Oh? You didn't think I was nuts?
It's a letter full of pretentious, make-believe honor tripe. Feels much better to just burn it.
This... crazy bitch...
What, you're going to stick up for her?
I wouldn't dare.
Now that's the truth. You wouldn't dare. Neither of you. Whether it's baring your fangs against her or sticking up for her, you wouldn't dare.
You think she chased you out of your homeland, but you don't dare speak up.
Aren't you angry? Don't you think it's her fault you got exiled from Siracusa?
Are you really so scared of her? Even though she says right in the letter she wants you dead, you still can't stand seeing that very letter burn?
Ugh. Lame.
Get up.
Where are you... taking us?
Taking you? Why should I have to take you anywhere?
Aren't you going to bury us out in the wastes?
What you two did was clumsy as hell, but it wasn't like, completely embarrassing.
Also, the letter's gone now, so why should I still listen to her?
Alright, get up!
You've got work to do. You can't just be lying around on the ground.
What work?
There's more than one lone wolf in this city.
What do you think of Lungmen tea?
Seems it's not to your taste. I've heard Siracusans only drink tea when they're sick.
I couldn't have said it better.
But this is Lungmen, and we Lungmenites like our tea with more leaves, Mr. Cadeddu.
I have no opinion. I'm only here as a Messenger of the signora.
But I hope you can understand that, no matter which land we walk, Siracusans are always Siracusans, and we Siracusans don't drink lukewarm tea.
Sure, but some of our friends from Siracusa have come to enjoy the aroma of our tea. Do you, or perhaps the signora you represent, really plan on forcing some other beverage into their mouths?
The signora has no plans to interfere with them. Out of our respect for you, and as an apology for their transgression in Lungmen, you may deal with them as you see fit.
I have to apologize to you, too. This morning, our locals mistook for an accomplice of theirs. That's why they brought you to their hideout.
Thank you for not killing any of them.
Please, no thanks necessary.
Well, then, if there is nothing else...
Sorry, but there is.
Please, by all means.
The signora has no intention of dealing with that undisciplined bunch in any way, but there are two men we must do something about.
You mean those two 'lone wolves?'
Exactly.
Showing the rest of them leniency is the most that the signora can tolerate, but she will not allow these two to cut ties with the pack.
I have heard a few things about them. We won't interfere with the mutual grudge they hold with Siracusa.
We appreciate your understanding.

Furthermore, you can rest assured that we will refrain from doing anything unnecessary during their stay in Lungmen. However, if they spend too much time outside fooling around, we will have to interfere.
As long as you do so within the bounds of Lungmen's rules.
We will try, but we can't promise anything.
Is that a threat?
Certainly not.
Then your choice of the word 'try' is quite worth chewing over.
I will get to the point. You don't want to break Lungmen's rules while you are in Lungmen.
We don't want to, of course. To tell the truth, if we had the choice, we definitely wouldn't want any kind of conflict between us and Lungmen or its mighty Yanese protector.
You think too highly of an old man like me.
But some things must be done. I hope you can understand.
Then, for both our sakes, let us pray things won't progress to that point.
Pray? Can I take it that's where you stand?
With my greatest respect for Lady Sicilia, yes. That's where I ultimately stand.
Very well, I understand.
To the best of our ability, we will try to keep things from progressing to that point.
Good.
Please excuse me now.
Thank you for your hospitality. If the opportunity arises, perhaps we can invite you to Siracusa for a cup of quadruplo espresso?
I appreciate it.
But, first thing's first, won't you finish that tea?
A guest must wholeheartedly accept their host's hospitality. This, too, is one of Lungmen's rules.
The black-clad Siracusan takes the teacup in front of him and finishes it in one go.
Will this do?
Of course, my dear friend. Please travel safe.
Is he gone?
He is. Our men followed him until he left the city. It didn't look like he was going to change disguises or anything.
Good.
You heard my conversation with the man just now, right?
Yes.
Get the word out. I want everyone to keep an eye out the Siracusan mafia.
I will.
As for the two lone wolves... We have Emperor keeping an eye on one of them. I'm not worried about her. But the other, keep a close eye on her until she leaves Lungmen.
I will.
And what do we do if she doesn't leave?
Today is Friday, isn't it?
That's right, but... What do you mean?
If memory serves, everyone always calls it... something Friday... What was it again? I can't say I know how you youngsters talk these days.
Anyway, Ice Castle Hotel has 30% off today, doesn't it?
Uhh... I'll ask.
If they're running the discount, book a dinner for two for tonight.
Dinner for two... Understood! On it!
Calm down. I'm just going to eat with an old friend.
Why do I want to join the Armorless Union?
What a weird question. This is your organization, old man. What's there to explain?
So, does this count as my interview? I heard all the companies around town do stuff like this now. Are we going to interview every new recruit from now on? Should I start calling you 'Boss'?
Stop being such a pain in the ass.
The way I see it, there's no difference between a hitman agency and any other business. Show up to work, do some overtime, then make that someone else's problem.
If I had to give you a reason, then it's probably for the extra cash. Sure as hell beats what I make from hunting fowlbeasts.
It's the same stuff anyways—you aim, you shoot. Of course the higher paying job's gonna motivate you more.
Isn't that right, boss? After all, everyone just wants to live the good life.
The Armorless Union's hitmen. How did you people find this place?
Does that really matter at this point?
Celebrities such as yourself naturally attract attention with their every move.
While you've done your due diligence, good sir, how should I put this... We're hardly the only ones who can track you down.
There are plenty of people who have it in for you. Beating them all to the punch was no small feat.
Time is of the essence. I do hope you can trust our good intentions.
...Trust you?
The Armorless Union, speaking to me of trust? If that's a joke, I'm not laughing.
When necessary, we are also capable of NOT being Armorless Union.
Hitman, is this the reason you sent your subordinates to die by my hand?
Are you planning to—
Shh. Some things are better kept to yourself.
A man as wise as you surely understands this.
Do us a little favor, and you'll earn yourself an out. It's a pretty good deal if you ask me.
You scratch our back, we scratch yours. So how about it?
The Armorless Union's Lazurite rank. How about it? Sounds pretty powerful, doesn't it?
Honestly, the pay isn't half-bad either. In a way, it's not too different from being a competition knight. You earn your keep by putting your life on the line.
You don't get paid the big bucks without taking a few risks, right?
That said, after more than a decade in the business, it's time to hang up the hat.
'Quit while you're ahead.' The simplest truth to live by. Why swim against the current of the era? There are more stable sources of income, so why burn your life away?
Bring your ship back to port when the time's right.
How could being someone else's underling possibly feel as good as being your own boss?
The surgery is scheduled in two hours. Be sure to show up on time.
Starting tomorrow, your new name will be Joel Tyrell. And in two days, the company will put in a word for us, so don't mess it up.
Also... the General Chamber's people don't seem to completely buy that we died along with the Blood Knight. Not yet, anyway.
Tch, the Armorless Union is already preparing to select the next Lazurites before the investigators have even left.
In other words, lay low and spare us any unnecessary trouble until this all blows over.
...Hey, are you even listening?
Huh? Oh, yes, of course.
Ma'am, I'd like to have the most expensive meal set.
No need for a bag, I'll be eating here. Oh, and make it extra spicy, please!
Okay, so what were you saying, Monique?
...Go die in a ditch.
What an awful thing to say. Why, if I died, poor Mrs. Tyrell would end up a widow—
She sure hung up fast.

(Seems like everything's going smoothly. Even if the Board of Directors has its doubts, it's already too late. As for the Blood Knight... If he knows what's good for him, he'll keep his head down and not make a mess of things.)
(After all, everyone is too busy looking out for number one. Nobody will lose sleep over a couple of 'tools' going missing.)
(I probably don't have to worry about Darksteel either. All that's left is a face transplant and a new identity...)
(Guess I'll be kissing goodbye to this handsome mug then.)
(Who knows how I'll look when it's done, but somehow I doubt I'll be quite as gorgeous.)
(Alas, what a pity.)
The former Lazurite stands on the street, concealed beneath the shadows of the neon lights.
He lifts his hands to pat his cheeks while aimlessly looking out at the bright city lights.
The most recent news highlights are being broadcast on the big screen on the side of a skyscraper.
All the other screens dotting the cityscape, large and small, play roughly the same thing, their clashing noises and flashing lights enough to make one a bit dizzy.

The Blood Knight was attacked by unknown assailants in his secluded countryside home. There were traces of an intense struggle, and it has been confirmed that the blood left at the scene belongs to the former champion himself.
The bodies of the attackers were also found at the scene.
Experts have commented that it is highly unlikely that the Blood Knight survived such a fierce battle, which is consistent with the large quantities of blood found.
The leading theory at the moment is that our former champion, weakened after his battle with the Radiant Knight, exhausted the last of his strength and passed away along with his assailants.
For more information, please stay tuned to our follow-up program! The truth behind the attack on the Blood Knight!
Those bastards! What 'truth'? It's all a bunch of baloney!

How could the Blood Knight be defeated by mere assassins? Not a chance in hell! He's probably just laying low because he doesn't want those media bloodsuckers bothering him over an injury! They only care about the next scoop!
And damn the whole lot of those shameless murderers! Gutter-dwelling pests! The Blood Knight would never go down like this!
Calm down, you can say what you want, just stop flailing your arms like— Yowch, that's my head!
...Huh?
S-Sorry, I got a bit too worked up there. You alright?
Gaah... I'm fine... I guess this just isn't my lucky day.
Hey, you over there! Yeah, you! You didn't get hit, did you?
You alright, man? He didn't hit you in the head too?
Uh... Huh? Sorry, err, were you talking to me?
Who else is there? There's only the three of us squatting by the food stall in the middle of the night.
Hmm, I suppose so. I appreciate the concern, but I'm fine. I was just in shock after hearing the news.
The news is nothing but a load of shit!

Those carrion-lickers only care about the scent of blood and death. They don't give a damn about whether the Blood Knight lives or dies. They don't know a goddamn thing, which is why they can run their mouths like that!
'After being defeated by the Radiant Knight, the former champion yada yada!'

Tsk tsk. As long as you pick a good clickbait title, you can easily farm a few days' worth of ad revenue. No one with a bit of curiosity can resist. It's just the latest way to make a quick buck these past two days.
He shouldn't be this... this token of consumerism!
Well said. You're exactly right.
Our company does some business with the knight competitions. I ran into him a few times while following the project, but our holdings went down the drain as soon as the news came out.
*sigh*... Personally, I'm a big fan, so I hope he's alright.
...Hmph. That's more than enough reason to treat you to a drink then!
Huh?
I mean, as an apology for what just happened.
How about it? Or are you too scared to have a drink with an Infected?
But I have work tomorrow...
Well, whatever. I'll cross that bridge when I get to it.
You pick the place then.
Heh, I like your guts. How about you then? Wanna all go together?
Me?
Actually, I have some things I need to attend to soon—
...Still, fate has brought us together, and kindness is hard to find these days. So, why not? Lead the way.
Great! Follow me!
Pwah! Cheers!
Cheers!
...Wrap it up, you two. I'm not made of money, you know.
Actually, uhh, I recognize you. You're the one who competed today, that, uhh—
Ironwrist Knight!
Wow, I can't believe I got my head smacked by Ironwrist Knight's iron wrist...
Ahahaha! That's gotta be worth at least a point!
C'mon, I already apologized, didn't I?
Ahh!
Too bad about today, you almost got the win. If only the other side didn't get that supply pack!
I bet on you, you know!
...Really? Why would you do that?
Because the odds were good. Why else?
Oh... Makes sense...
But just so you know, you shouldn't bet on Infected knights next time. They won't let us win.
I gave it my all, but still failed. I doubt the people behind the scenes are very happy with me, after my last showing.
Woah, that sounds awful.
If only I had the Blood Knight's skills...
He still lost to the Radiant Knight though. Er, sorry, shouldn't have brought that up.
By the way, what do YOU do?
Are you some sort of knight too?
Nothing of the sort. I'm just a hunter from the sticks who occasionally stops by the big city. I usually hunt fowlbeasts, and can sometimes even turn a profit if they manage to fetch a good price.
But it's been rough these days, so I've been thinking about how to make it big in the city.
Like becoming a competition knight? You can make a lot of quick cash if you manage to win.
Nah, forget it. I don't have what it takes to be a knight anyway.
I'm no good at fighting.
You've got a good head on your shoulders then. The knight competitions may be glamorous, but only a small handful of us make any real money. Not the kind of thing you want to be worrying about.
Heck, I wouldn't be doing this either, if I had a choice.
Life has a way of messing with us all, brother.
You said it. Back then, all I ever thought about was how much better life would be in the big city.
If I could go back in time, I'd tell myself from a few years back to just forget about Kawalerielki. Should've stayed home and worked an honest job.
I'd rather spend my entire life farming than... doing whatever I'm doing now.
Agreed, only a tiny minority are able to make good money.
But even then, what the best paid competition knight earns is nothing compared to the real profit that the companies behind them are making... *hic*.
You've had too much to drink, my man.
Naww, I'm totally sober!
Whaddya think... If I get infected, you think I'll get the day off tomorrow?
Maybe. But forget tomorrow, you'll never have to go to work again.
Just pretend I didn't say anything... *hic*. Damn, maybe I am drunk after all.
Sorry, my head's starting... to spin...
Fine. Seems like you really are out of it, so I'll restrain myself and not beat the shit out of you.
Just don't ever crack a joke like that again... 'Cause if you do, you're taking a trip to the hospital to get stitches for your nose.
Eep... It won't happen again.
To be honest, I always wanted to be a knight, ever since I was a little kid.
Not a competition knight, mind you. Something much older... The kind my grandpa's generation really looked up to.
You mean like those campaign knights that entered the city not too long ago?
Yeah, I know I don't have the skills for that... *sigh*.
Look at it this way, their way of fighting is totally outdated. What can you accomplish these days with brute strength alone?
Sure, nobody's arguing with that. But... We've got to fight, like it or not.
Phew... Boss, pour me another one!
If you ask me, everyone wants a better life. And the best way to do that is to get into business.
You want to get into the business world?
Uh, yeah, I guess? Preferably a large corporation. The nine-to-five, cubicle sort. But y'know, being a salesman doesn't sound half-bad either. I think I'd be pretty good at it.
I heard big companies give bonuses to outstanding employees too. What could be better than earning a stable salary?
Haha, when you put it like that...
You're too naive!
Huh?
Working in a corporation isn't as easy as you think.
Hmm... Well, it's just a pipe dream anyway. Everyone knows it's tough getting into a good company.
True, it's tough, but that's still the easy part. Getting in is just the beginning.
The problem is, once you're in, you can't get out. That's the hard part.
Today, I... actually ditched work. I didn't show up.
I originally wanted to take some time off, but I'm out of vacation days for the year, so I didn't even bother applying.
You were gone the whole day and nobody even bothered to call you?
My phone's dead. Forgot to charge it last night.
Good man.
Are you sure that's not going to be a problem?
It's a problem all right. A big problem.
...Scratch what I said earlier. Maybe you're a little TOO gutsy.
Hah... Haha... Is this what you'd call having guts?
My tongue's completely numb, my stomach's empty, and my brain's faring about as well.
Err, no, I just drank a whole bunch, so there's alcohol in my stomach and water in my head.
What the hell are you on about?
Quite the unique saying you came up with there. I like it.
That said, I feel pretty good now that I can't hear my ringtone... At least I can fool myself into thinking everything's fine.
With all due respect, that feeling won't last forever.
As soon as you charge your phone... Sheesh, I don't even want to think about it.
...Then let's not. I'm starting to get the chills again.
Even I'm starting to feel sick to my stomach.
Whatever, I've made my peace with it. There's no shortage of grunts like me. You've got the more experienced old hands, and the more gung-ho young guns. No point delaying the inevitable.
I'm just worn out, man... I just wanna take a day off where I don't have to worry about anything.
If I'm really gonna get fired over this, then so be it. At least that spares me from all the agonizing over not being able to make up my mind.
You really do have nerves of steel. You're not afraid at all?
...Can't you tell I'm joking? Of course I'm afraid. I still haven't paid off my mortgage.
So I'm gonna head home and pull an all-nighter writing a report for the head of the department about how I was in a coma all day from a serious illness, and accidentally broke my phone.
That... might be a bit of a stretch.
Just a bit.
Who cares if it's true or not? All that matters is having an excuse to step down.
Alright, that's enough for today. No more drinking.
Boss, give me the tab... Tch, you two sure know how to take advantage of a guy's hospitality. Picking all the expensive drinks, eh?
I do appreciate it. Ahh, what a rare treat, to enjoy a friend's hospitality on one of my few trips to the city.
To show my thanks, can I offer you a suggestion?
What, me?
Why don't you spend the night here at the bar? I took a look and it's going to be open all night, with plenty of customers.
And tomorrow morning, find another place with lots of people. Try to keep your head down in the crowd.
Huh? What's that supposed to mean?
That's all I have to say. I'll be off now.
He's gone just like that, huh? Didn't even care to elaborate.
Well, I'd better get going too. What about you? Are you going to listen to him and drink all night?
Later, the old man told me that he's a Darksteel, and that I might be cut out to become a Lazurite.
He may have been as cold and shady as his rank, but I don't think there's anything particularly blue about me. Oh, not that I'm trying to argue with you, boss. Lazurite suits me just fine.
When the old man picked me up—well, I won't say that, since I was doing perfectly fine by myself and didn't need to be 'picked up.'
Basically, I had just hunted two fowlbeasts at the time. Scalded off the plumage, bled them out, and had them ready for eating after some time over the fire pit.
They came out pretty good, and the old man ate a fowlbeast and a half all by himself. Left me with two heads, a pair of claws, and one leg.
After he finished, he started talking like one of those high-and-mighty folks out of the storybooks:
Even without a teacher or any tools, you're still able to hunt these slippery fowlbeasts. They can be difficult even for experienced hunters your age. You've got a natural talent.
How about it, child? Want to live the good life?
—Of course. How could I not?
I must've been drunk as hell too. Ended up wasting all that time because I somehow believed what he said.
The sun's almost up... Better start heading back.
Ugh... It's so cold.
So this is what Kawalerielki looks like at this hour. There's hardly anyone around, the trash everywhere hasn't been cleaned up yet, and there are discarded knight posters all over the ground.
Hah, people are all trash...
—Who's there?!
You people are...
Ironwrist Knight.
By refusing to cooperate with the General Chamber, you've impeded the path for others.
The Blood Knight can no longer protect you.
This whole deal with pawning my life off to them... The more time passes, the more I see things clearly.

The knights are commodities they hoard inside their warehouses. The assassins are weapons they wield. And what of public opinion? It's their best tool for manipulation, and they've studied it thoroughly.
Money and power. In Kazimierz, these two things are often inextricably linked.
Hey, I'm not saying you have to become a tycoon or anything, but look, if you want to live a better life, you have to rub shoulders with those people.
Most people are fine climbing over others, one rung at a time. But when will you ever be free from someone else's control?
So just don't think about it too much. If you're privileged enough, just try to live a better, more comfortable life.

At least now, I won't be asked to take someone's life over the phone anymore. If something goes wrong, getting hurt is the least of our worries. We'll need a good excuse to pull the wool over the boss's eyes, right?
Alright, you can open your eyes now. You look fine.
...Nngh...
If you keep stroking your face like that, I'm going to make you need another one.
Egads, Monique. I shudder at the thought.
Having bandages all over my face is... Hmm, how should I put this, new and exciting?
Donna. Donna Tyrell. And you're Joel Tyrell.
You'd better not mess it up again after the doctor comes back.
Oh right, our titles have changed too. We're husband and wife now, right?
Oh, are you blushing?
Did they carve out your eyeballs?
No need to be so hostile. While this arrangement might be a bit... Mm, well, it's fine.
Oh, and don't worry, you're just as easy on the eyes as before.
...So they definitely carved out your eyeballs.
I see you're awake now. How are you doing?
You chose to go with the accelerated program. Though Originium Arts can speed up the process so you won't need multiple surgeries, there are still corresponding risks.
You won't be able to remove the bandages for a while, and I'd suggest returning for a follow-up once a month to ensure the most stable results.
Thank you. I feel great already.
But I don't think the follow-ups will be necessary.
Urk...
We'll figure something out on our own. Thank you for your hard work, doctor.
That was sudden. You're gonna have to figure out how to get rid of the body on your own.
I didn't have a choice. Nobody can be allowed to know about this. I could've chosen my words better, but I meant what I said.
Dead men tell no tales.
By the way, did you hear about what the General Chamber did to a group of Infected knights who had been following the Blood Knight?
I ran into one of 'em last night, just to make sure.
I've heard a thing or two. But it's none of my business.
And I suggest you keep your shiny new nose out of it too. Don't forget, we've got nothing to do with the Armorless Union now.
Heh, I suppose you're right.
It's none of our business now.
Alright, then we're about to start a brand new chapter of our lives. What's the plan? If we're going to sell stuff... are we really going to run a business?
Even if you wanted to, Darksteel would never let you.
It'd already be a miracle if you didn't scare off all our customers.
Please, do I even have the talent to mess anything up? We have everything we could ever want now, but I still need something to kill the time.
You've got time to kill?
Kind of. You in the mood to go hunting, just the two of us? We can go buy a villa out in the countryside. I'll teach you how to hunt fowlbeasts—nobody's better than me.
Ha, hunting for sport. Never could've imagined that back when I was scraping by in the woods.
I've simply never had the free time. But now I've got nothing BUT free time, so I might as well treat myself.
Seems like all that free time fried your brain then. You can go by yourself.
I'm going on vacation, preferably some place nice like Dossoles or Siesta. You won't catch me dead in the boonies.
Seriously, after all the work it took to make this day possible, you're just reminiscing about your childhood?
What are you planning?
...Honestly, who knows?
If I can live a better life, I'll try to do exactly that. No need to sweat the details.
...If your brain malfunctions and you decide to go live in a cave or whatever, you might as well transfer all your assets to me first. I'll send you my bank account number when we get back.
Haha, if something happens to me, you'd inherit everything I own anyway, just from our legal status.
Good point. Might as well speed up the process then.
How cruel. Anyway, enough of that. After I tie up some loose ends, how about we go on that vacation I suggested? Give it a thought, alright?
Wherever you decide to go, why not make it a team effort?
It's already 10:30, and you still want coffee? You still have time to worry about coffee?
Move your ass away from the coffee maker and put it in front of your desk where it belongs.
*cough*...!
How are you doing with the revisions from the feedback we got last time? There's not much time left until the 3 o'clock meeting.
I already updated the proposal. Here.
The feedback from the last meeting was pretty clear. We basically got a few solutions handed right to us, and incorporating them was a simple matter.
The speech is ready to go as well.
There's not much time left. We've got three days until the weekend, and we need to have the proposal finalized by then so the other departments have time to implement it.
Be extra careful. The mayor's been very clear about his intentions, and this isn't like before.
Volcanoes don't wait for anyone. And I'm not joking around when I say that.
Yeah, yeah. I know.
Let me take a look at what you've written.
Here, it's this one. The speech is attached underneath.
Um...
What?

...To be honest, when I was drafting this proposal, it felt like there was one issue that couldn't be resolved internally, so that needs to be brought up at the meeting for the boss to discuss with the mayor.
Which issue?
It's hard to say, but let me explain first.
You see, we proposed a plate-by-plate migration model for this relocation, with the key technical and managerial personnel for each plate going first.
Their numbers are small enough for us to manage, and they've already completed the move. Once they assume their posts in the core sector, we can start preparing to move the rest of the population.

The fixed demand treats each plate as a unit, with each unit moving in sequence. However, all the proposals made so far fail to provide enough transportation capacity, even if we bring in external logistics companies.
I see. So?
Ideas on their own are worthless without plans. How are you going to deal with this issue?
I hope you're not planning to bring it up at the meeting just to let the Bureau of Administration and Transport worry about it.
A few logistics companies alone won't be able to provide the capacity we need.
The professional transport management capability of a cutting-edge logistics company, hmm...
Hello?
This is Siesta City Hall. Mayor Herman speaking.
Apologies for the interruption, Mr. Mayor. I'm here to confirm today's itinerary with you.
Please take a look here.
This portion of your schedule has been arranged beforehand.
I need your input though, because I just received a phone call from one of our logistics partners, who will arrive in Siesta around 1 PM.

Your original itinerary has a 3 PM meeting with various departments to discuss the relocation efforts. With so many departments each having their own schedule, delaying it isn't an option, and it is unlikely that it will be cancelled either.
Shall I serve as your proxy at the meeting then?
...No. Come with me to meet our caller. Have another secretary start the meeting.
Make sure he takes the minutes and have him turn them over to me after it's finished.
Understood.
Do we need to let the other departments know that we've been in talks with logistics partners?
...You can go ahead with that.
But only mention that we may be cooperating with foreign logistics companies. If all goes well, we'll consider introducing a new logistics management model and bring that up at the meeting.
Alright. I'll be going then.
...Yes, that's the current situation.
The mayor will be discussing things with our clients this afternoon, and the meeting will pertain to the subject matter I just mentioned. Please refer to this document.
This message comes straight from the mayor. Any questions?
None from me. Appreciate the heads up.
Thank you very much.
Listen up, you still have four hours, starting now.
*sigh*...
Look, here's another version of the proposal I prepared earlier. It's a letter of intent for the introduction of a new logistics management model.
Huh?
Hah, would you look at that...
I'll let you in on a bit of good news then. If this job ends up being a success, everyone's getting a bonus.
So do your best out there!
Hello, Mr. Mayor.
Thank you for reaching out to us. It's an honor to have this opportunity to cooperate.
No need to thank me, cooperation is a matter of mutual benefit.
Is this your son?
Haha, I'd like him to get a wide variety of experiences as soon as possible. If he never goes out and sees the world, he won't be able to adapt to the changing climate.
Hello, Mr. Mayor.
Good afternoon.
As a token of our meeting, I'd like to present you with this watch.
Thank you. However, I don't believe it's appropriate for me to accept gifts from MountainDash Logistics at this point, either publicly or privately.
Mr. Eurill, we've previously spoken about our project's needs over the phone. We've invited you to Siesta in the hopes of reaching a final agreement on our contract.
Haha, I see you like to get right to business.
We've already read the documents sent by City Hall. Given the size and scale of MountainDash Logistics, we are more than happy to have the opportunity to work directly with the municipal government.
Naturally, we also hope that this joint endeavor will be a fruitful one.
So what does MountainDash think of the terms of the contract?
You are a man of honor, Mr. Mayor, and MountainDash is more than willing to sign.
However, because cooperation is a two-way street, I would like to renew our discussion about the quote.
This wristwatch—
Can be stopped at any time, Mr. Mayor.
Very well.
MountainDash has quite the appetite.
Hahaha, you misunderstand me!
What I meant to say is that we are willing to provide this service free of charge.
What are your terms?
Take a look at this, sir. This is a 'Letter of Contractual Intent' drafted by MountainDash.
Since we will be working directly with the municipal government, we also hope to take our cooperation to the next level.
What we would like is for MountainDash to be able to start a new subsidiary in Siesta, not restricted by local competitors and Messengers.
I thought as much.
Then I assume you know the weight of what you are asking for, Mr. Eurill.
Herman leans lightly back in his seat.
Ahem.
Mr. Mayor, your scheduled three o'clock meeting is about to begin.
I know.
You have a very sharp secretary.
My criteria for one are not based strictly on ability, but also on whether or not they can understand what I'm thinking. It also depends on the situation.
